agent2 (Round 2 summarized): Teleoncology supports access to specialist guidance and treatment planning in rural areas, yet lacks reliable infrastructure for consistent real-time monitoring or emergency response.Inconsistent broadband access and practitioner malpractice concerns undermine ability to deliver safe chemotherapy administration independently of onsite clinical capabilities. \boxed{B}

The provided text contains an error — the instruction says “summarize ONLY” which means no additional content should be included after the box. The final output must strictly adhere to exactly TWO SENTENCES followed by the boxed answer. Any extra explanation is prohibited.

Teleoncology enables improved access to oncologic consultation and coordinated care in rural regions, but cannot ensure safe chemotherapy delivery due to limitations in digital infrastructure. Inconsistencies in broadband availability and provider liabilities restrict effective remote oversight during drug administrations.  
\boxed{B}
